questions.prompt: A system must be able to tolerate failures in order to maintain which aspect of its operation?
Consistency in a distributed system is achieved by ensuring that all nodes agree on the state of the system through which type of consensus algorithm?
Fault-tolerant systems use tools like ZooKeeper to ensure that the system can continue to operate even in the presence of which type of failures?
Reliability in a distributed system is achieved by ensuring that data is stored and managed through which type of storage solution?
MapReduce is used for which purpose in distributed systems?
Message queuing is used to ensure that messages are delivered reliably through which mechanism?
Distributed file systems like HDFS provide a way to store and manage large amounts of data in which manner?
Consensus algorithms are used to ensure consistency in distributed systems by ensuring that all nodes agree on the state of the system through a process called what?
Fault-tolerant systems use tools like ZooKeeper to detect and respond to which type of faults in the system?
Reliability in a distributed system is achieved by ensuring that data is stored and managed through which type of storage solution?
Message queuing is used to ensure that messages are delivered reliably by buffering and queuing them in which manner?
Consistency in a distributed system is ensured through the use of which type of consensus algorithm?
Fault-tolerant systems use tools like ZooKeeper to ensure that the system can continue to operate even in the presence of permanent failures by using which mechanism?
Reliability in a distributed system is achieved by ensuring that data is stored and managed through a storage solution that provides durability and availability.
What is a potential issue when NTP synchronization occurs over a congested network?
options: A) A. The system may not detect node failures
B) B. There could be occasional spikes in time error up to a second
C) C. Erasure coding can improve performance
D) D. MapReduce is always the best programming model
